read.dek: On June 22, 2025, the morning after Trump orders strikes on three Iranian nuclear facilities, Jiang turns the Iran war into a three-player game. Iran, Israel, and Trump do not want the same world, but in his model they all want the same American mistake: ground troops in Iran.
read.thesis: The episode's strange force is the inversion of strength. America is the hegemon, but escalation dominance makes it predictable. Iran can provoke the stronger actor into harsher moves. Israel can use an American-Iranian quagmire to remove America from the Middle East. Trump can give the American Empire what it wants in order to split it from America First. Game theory explains why the trap works; prophecy explains why the players may accept the end-of-the-world stakes.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: The good news is not comfort. The bad news is that the world is approaching its end; the good news is that this morning Jiang will explain how it ends. The whole episode turns on one question: will America use ground troops in Iran? Bombing can begin the war, but ground troops would, in Jiang's model, lose it. They would end the American Empire, ignite an American civil war, and still look inevitable once the game is set in motion.

beats.summary: The source closes not with doctrine but with exposure: Jiang is suddenly internet famous and has to keep teaching while protecting his family.
